assessment.title: JoraIQ Company-Based Assessment
assessment.description: Placement-readiness assessment covering DSA, SQL, OOPs and Core CS MCQs.

questions.title: ServiceNow Incident Dependency Resolution
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
ServiceNow manages IT incidents. Some incidents cannot be resolved until other related incidents are resolved first. For example, Incident A depends on Incident B, and Incident B depends on Incident C. So C must be resolved before B, and B before A. The operations team wants to know whether all incidents can be resolved, and if yes, in what valid order. However, sometimes dependency loops may exist, such as A depends on B, B depends on C, and C depends on A. In this case, resolution is impossible.

Problem Statement
You are given n incidents numbered from 0 to n-1. You are also given a list of dependencies where each dependency [a, b] means incident a depends on incident b. Return a valid order in which incidents can be resolved. If it is impossible to resolve all incidents due to circular dependencies, return an empty list.

Input Example: n = 6 dependencies = [[2,1], [3,2], [4,2], [5,3]] Output Example: [0, 1, 2, 4, 3, 5] Other valid orders are also acceptable.

open_test_cases.expected: Any valid order: 0 before 1 before 2 and 3 before 4 before 5

hidden_test_cases.expected: Any valid order containing 0 and 4, with 1 before 2 before 3

test_cases.expected_output: Any valid order: 0 before 1 before 2 and 3 before 4 before 5
Any valid order containing 0 and 4, with 1 before 2 before 3

questions.title: Amazon Delivery Route Optimization with Dynamic Traffic
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
Amazon delivery partners need to deliver packages across multiple locations. Each route between two locations has a travel time. However, due to traffic or road conditions, some roads have different travel costs. The delivery team wants to find the minimum delivery time from the warehouse to all customer locations.

Problem Statement
You are given n locations numbered from 0 to n-1. Location 0 is the warehouse. You are given a list of roads [u, v, time], which means there is a road from location u to location v that takes time minutes. Return the minimum time required to reach every location from the warehouse. If any location cannot be reached, return -1 for that location.

Input Example: n = 5 roads = [[0,1,10],[0,2,3],[2,1,4],[1,3,2],[2,3,8],[3,4,7]] Output: [0,7,3,9,16]

questions.title: Commvault Backup Deduplication System
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
Commvault backup systems store large amounts of enterprise data. To save storage, repeated file chunks should not be stored again. Each file is broken into chunks. Every chunk has a unique hash. The system receives backup requests from multiple machines. You need to calculate how much new storage is required after deduplication.

Problem Statement
You are given n backup files. Each file contains a list of chunk hashes. A chunk should be stored only once globally, even if it appears in multiple files. For each file, return how many new unique chunks were added to the backup system when that file was processed.

Input: files = [["a1","b2","c3"],["b2","d4"],["a1","e5","f6"],["g7","d4"]] Output: [3,1,2,1]

Twist to Make It Harder
Inside a single file, the same chunk may appear multiple times. Example: ["a1", "a1", "b2"]. This file should add only 2 new chunks, not 3.
constraints: Total number of chunks across all files <= 500000

hidden_test_cases.input: 100000 files, total chunks=500000, many repeated chunks
hidden_test_cases.expected: Correct new unique chunk count per file within time limit

test_cases.input: 100000 files, total chunks=500000, many repeated chunks
test_cases.expected_output: Correct new unique chunk count per file within time limit

questions.title: Autodesk Versioned Design File History
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
Autodesk products like AutoCAD and Fusion 360 maintain multiple versions of design files. A user may create a file, update it many times, and later ask: â€œWhat was the value of this design parameter at version 7?â€ You need to build a system that supports updating a design parameter and retrieving the value of a parameter at a specific version.

Problem Statement
Design a versioned key-value system. The system should support set(key, value), which stores the value for the key in the current version, and get(key, version), which returns the value of the key at the given version. Every set operation creates a new version number starting from 1. If the key did not exist at that version, return "NULL".

Operations: set("wall_height", 10) // version 1 set("door_width", 4) // version 2 set("wall_height", 12) // version 3 get("wall_height", 2) get("wall_height", 3) get("door_width", 1) Output: 10 12 NULL

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Key not present at earlier version

hidden_test_cases.expected: All get operations correct within time limit

test_cases.purpose: Key not present at earlier version

test_cases.expected_output: All get operations correct within time limit

questions.title: Amazon Fraud Detection Using Sliding Window
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
Amazon wants to detect suspicious customer activity. A customer is considered suspicious if they make more than k transactions within any continuous time window of t seconds. You are given a list of transactions sorted by time. Each transaction contains customerId and timestamp. Find all customers who became suspicious.

Problem Statement
Given a list of transactions sorted by timestamp, return all customer IDs who made more than k transactions within any t seconds window.

Input: transactions = [["C1",1],["C2",2],["C1",3],["C1",5],["C2",6],["C1",7]] k = 3 t = 6 Output: ["C1"]

Important Boundary Rule
If the problem says more than k transactions within any t seconds window, then for timestamps start and end, the window is valid when end - start <= t. So if t = 6, transactions at time 1 and 7 are inside the same window.

hidden_test_cases.expected: Correct suspicious customer list within time limit

test_cases.expected_output: Correct suspicious customer list within time limit

questions.title: Amazon Customer Delivery Promise Breach Analysis
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Amazon promises customers a delivery date when they place an order. In reality, one customer order can have many items, and those items may be shipped in different packages from different fulfilment centres. A customer may feel that the delivery promise was broken if the final package of the order arrives after the promised date. The operations and customer experience teams want to identify customers who are repeatedly facing promise breaches, but they also want the logic to be fair: cancelled orders should be excluded, split shipments should be handled correctly, and only customers with enough delivered order history should be flagged.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to flag customers as High-Risk Customer Experience Cases. A customer should be flagged if: (1) they have at least 5 delivered orders in the last 90 days, (2) at least 40% of those delivered orders were delivered after the promised delivery timestamp, (3) the average delay on late orders is more than 2 days, (4) cancelled orders, returned-only orders and orders with no delivered shipment should not be considered in the delivered-order denominator, (5) an order is considered delivered only when all non-cancelled items have been delivered, (6) for split shipments, the actual delivery timestamp of the order is the latest delivered shipment timestamp, and (7) delivery exactly on or before the promised timestamp is not late.

Tables
customers(customer_id INT, customer_name VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, customer_segment VARCHAR, signup_date DATE) orders(order_id INT, customer_id INT, order_date DATE, order_status VARCHAR, promised_delivery_ts TIMESTAMP, order_value DECIMAL) order_items(order_id INT, item_id INT, product_id INT, item_status VARCHAR, item_value DECIMAL) shipments(shipment_id INT, order_id INT, shipped_ts TIMESTAMP, delivered_ts TIMESTAMP, shipment_status VARCHAR, fulfilment_center_id INT) fulfilment_centers(fulfilment_center_id INT, city VARCHAR, region VARCHAR)

questions.title: Commvault Backup Failure Streak and Recovery Risk
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Commvault runs backup jobs for many enterprise clients. Each client has multiple servers, and each server can be attached to a backup policy. Some jobs succeed, some fail, and some are missed because the server was unavailable. The infrastructure team does not want to simply count total failures. They want to know which active servers are currently risky because their latest backup health shows a continuing failure streak. This is important because an old failure streak that was later recovered should not create panic, but a current unresolved streak should be escalated immediately.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to return active servers that are currently under Critical Backup Risk. A server should be flagged if: (1) it belongs to an active client, (2) the server itself is active, (3) the server is attached to a DAILY backup policy, (4) within the last 30 days it has a latest consecutive FAILED streak of at least 3 backup runs, (5) the latest backup run for the server is still FAILED, (6) a SUCCESS after a failure streak breaks the risk, (7) a MISSED job is not a success and should be shown in history, but it should break a consecutive FAILED-only streak, and (8) the query should return only the latest unresolved failure streak, not an older recovered streak.

Tables
clients(client_id INT, client_name VARCHAR, account_status VARCHAR) servers(server_id INT, client_id INT, server_name VARCHAR, environment VARCHAR, is_active BOOLEAN) backup_policies(policy_id INT, policy_name VARCHAR, backup_frequency VARCHAR, is_enabled BOOLEAN) server_policy_map(server_id INT, policy_id INT, effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE) backup_jobs(job_id INT, server_id INT, policy_id INT, scheduled_start_ts TIMESTAMP, completed_ts TIMESTAMP, status VARCHAR, data_size_gb DECIMAL) backup_error_logs(job_id INT, error_code VARCHAR, error_message VARCHAR)

questions.title: Autodesk License Usage Anomaly and Seat Optimization
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Autodesk sells software licenses to companies. A company may buy many seats, assign only some seats to employees, and those employees may or may not actually use the product. Usage also happens through sessions, and one user can have multiple sessions in a day. The customer success team wants to identify accounts where the customer is paying for seats that are not being used well. This can help the sales/customer success team recommend a better license plan, training, or seat reduction before renewal.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to flag customers for License Optimization Review. A customer should be flagged if: (1) they have at least 10 purchased seats on an active paid contract, (2) their average daily active users in the last 30 days is less than 50% of purchased seats, (3) at least 20% of active licensed users have average daily usage below 10 minutes, (4) customers with zero usage in the last 30 days should also be flagged, (5) trial contracts and internal/test users should be excluded, (6) usage should be counted only for users who were assigned a license during the usage date, and (7) multiple sessions by the same user on the same day should count as one daily active user but their minutes should be summed for engagement calculation.

Tables
customers(customer_id INT, customer_name VARCHAR, industry VARCHAR, account_status VARCHAR) license_contracts(contract_id INT, customer_id INT, plan_type VARCHAR, purchased_seats INT, contract_status VARCHAR, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, is_trial BOOLEAN) users(user_id INT, customer_id INT, user_email VARCHAR, user_status VARCHAR, is_internal_user BOOLEAN) license_assignments(assignment_id INT, contract_id INT, user_id INT, assigned_from DATE, assigned_to DATE) usage_sessions(session_id INT, user_id INT, product_id INT, session_start_ts TIMESTAMP, session_end_ts TIMESTAMP, minutes_used INT) products(product_id INT, product_name VARCHAR, product_family VARCHAR)

questions.title: ServiceNow Incident Workflow Engine
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
ServiceNow handles different types of IT service tickets such as Incident, Service Request, Change Request and Problem Ticket. Each ticket type has a different workflow. The company wants to build a workflow engine where new ticket types can be added in the future without changing the existing workflow execution logic.

Problem Statement
The system should support creating different ticket types, moving each ticket to its next valid state, preventing invalid transitions, returning the current state of a ticket, and adding a new ticket type without changing existing core engine code.

starter_code.python: # Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.javascript: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.java: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.cpp: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.

questions.title: Amazon Multi-Mode Payment Processing System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Amazon supports different payment modes such as Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, Wallet, Gift Card and Cash on Delivery. Each payment mode has different rules. The system should allow new modes like BNPL, EMI or International Card without changing the core checkout flow.

Problem Statement
The system should support processing payment using different payment modes, validating payment before processing, returning payment success or failure, applying payment-specific rules, and adding new payment modes without modifying checkout logic.

starter_code.python: # Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.javascript: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.java: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.cpp: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.

questions.title: Autodesk Design Export System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Autodesk tools allow users to export design files into PDF, DWG, DXF, STL, OBJ and PNG. Each format has different export behaviour. In the future, Autodesk may add SVG, IFC, STEP or GLTF. The system should allow adding new export formats without modifying the core design file class.

Problem Statement
The system should support exporting a design file into different formats, each format having its own logic, returning success/failure response, handling unsupported formats and adding new export formats without changing existing design file logic.

questions.title: OOPs - Payment System Design
questions.prompt: An e-commerce platform supports many payment types and wants to add future methods without changing checkout logic. Which design is best?

options.label: B
options.text: Separate methods inside checkout for each payment type

options.label: C
options.text: PaymentMethod interface with separate classes for each type

options.label: D
options.text: Store payment type as string and use switch-case

questions.explanation: Interface and separate classes support polymorphism and Open/Closed Principle.

questions.title: OOPs - Workflow State Management
questions.prompt: Different ticket types have workflows and invalid transitions must be blocked. Which pattern is most suitable?
questions.explanation: State Pattern is suitable when behavior depends on current state and transitions are controlled.

questions.title: OOPs - Export System
questions.prompt: DesignFile should not change when a new export format is added. Which principle is followed?
questions.explanation: The system is open for extension but closed for modification.

questions.title: OOPs - Bad Design Detection
questions.prompt: CheckoutService contains if(paymentType == 'UPI') else if(paymentType == 'CARD'). What is the biggest issue?
questions.explanation: Every new payment mode requires modifying checkout.

questions.title: Computer Networks - DNS Issue
questions.prompt: A user can access a website using its IP but not domain name. What is most likely wrong?
questions.explanation: If IP works but domain does not, DNS is likely failing.

questions.title: Computer Networks - TCP vs UDP
questions.prompt: A video calling app prefers low latency over perfect reliability. Which protocol is more suitable?
questions.explanation: UDP is preferred for real-time traffic where retransmission delay is undesirable.

questions.title: Computer Networks - Load Balancer
questions.prompt: An app wants to distribute high traffic across backend servers. Which component should be used?
questions.explanation: A load balancer distributes incoming traffic across servers.

questions.title: Operating System - Deadlock
questions.prompt: Four processes wait for resources held by each other. Which condition is directly responsible?
questions.explanation: Circular wait is a necessary condition for deadlock.

questions.title: Operating System - Memory Management
questions.prompt: Inactive pages are moved from RAM to disk while many programs run. Which concept is used?
questions.explanation: Virtual memory uses disk as an extension of RAM.

questions.title: Operating System - Process vs Thread
questions.prompt: A web server creates lightweight units within the same application memory space. What is being used?
questions.explanation: Threads share process memory and are lightweight.

questions.title: Cloud Computing - Auto Scaling
questions.prompt: A site automatically adds servers during sale traffic and removes them after. Which cloud feature is used?
questions.explanation: Auto Scaling adjusts compute resources based on demand.

questions.title: Cloud Computing - High Availability
questions.prompt: A company deploys across multiple availability zones. What is the main goal?
questions.explanation: Multiple availability zones improve fault tolerance.

questions.title: Cloud Computing - Storage Choice
questions.prompt: A company stores large volumes of images, videos and backups without relational querying. Which storage is suitable?
questions.explanation: Object storage is suitable for unstructured files.

questions.title: Network Security - Phishing
questions.prompt: An email pretends to be IT support and asks for password reset via suspicious link. What attack is this?
questions.explanation: Phishing tricks users by pretending to be trusted.

questions.title: Network Security - SQL Injection
questions.prompt: A login form directly inserts user input into SQL without parameterization. What vulnerability is likely?
questions.explanation: Unsafe input can execute as part of SQL.

questions.title: Network Security - Least Privilege
questions.prompt: A developer account only accesses database tables required for the project. Which principle is followed?
questions.explanation: Least privilege gives only necessary access.

questions.title: Computer Architecture - Cache
questions.prompt: A CPU repeatedly accesses the same small set of instructions and data faster because they are stored close to CPU. Which component is responsible?
questions.explanation: Cache stores frequently accessed data near CPU.

questions.title: Computer Architecture - Pipelining
questions.prompt: A processor overlaps fetch, decode, execute and write-back stages. Which concept is this?
questions.explanation: Pipelining improves instruction throughput.

questions.title: MS Excel - Lookup Issue
questions.prompt: VLOOKUP fetches salary by employee ID, but employee ID is not first column in selected range. What issue occurs?

options.label: B
options.text: VLOOKUP may fail because lookup column must be first

questions.explanation: VLOOKUP searches the first column of the table array.

questions.title: MS Excel - Duplicate Customer Records
questions.prompt: Transaction data has the same customer many times. The analyst wants total spend per customer. Which Excel feature is most suitable?
questions.explanation: Pivot Tables group records and summarize totals.
